front: What is a star, in one sentence?
back: A star is a massive ball of hot gas where gravity inward is balanced by energy from nuclear fusion outward.

front: Why is stellar mass so important?
back: Mass controls core pressure and temperature, which determines a star's lifespan and how it dies.
The long stable phase when a star fuses hydrogen in its core.
An expanded late-life star that is often cooler at the surface but much larger in size.

front: Why can iron trigger collapse in massive stars?
back: Because fusion at that stage no longer supplies enough energy to keep core pressure supporting the star.
A powerful stellar explosion after core collapse that disperses heavy elements into space.
Two stars gravitationally bound in orbit around a shared center of mass.
Variable stars truly change emitted light; twinkling is mainly atmospheric distortion seen from Earth.
A huge gravitational system of stars, gas, dust, and dark matter, often containing millions to trillions of stars.
The Milky Way is a barred spiral galaxy, and the Sun lies in one of its spiral arms.
The boundary around a black hole beyond which nothing, including light, can escape.
A predicted quantum process through which black holes slowly lose energy and can eventually evaporate.
